title: docs: make clear that "remote client" implies Mac

comments.body:
You can run `podman --remote` on Linux ? Must be missing something here.

Maybe you meant that macOS implies remote ? In that case, I would agree.

What I mean is that the docs mention a lot that "feature/option A is not available for the remote client".  It's not clear to most readers that this implies the Mac client, so I think it should be made explicit.

OK, so when you say "implies" you mean "includes". Then agreed.

(I guess WSL2 is technically **Linux**, but most users wouldn't know)

> OK, so when you say "implies" you mean "includes". Then agreed.

Yes, thanks for clarifying :)

> (I guess WSL2 is technically **Linux**, but most users wouldn't know)

Agreed.

We have another set of confusion, and that is those users that do "lima podman" or "podman machine ssh podman".

_Then_ we could argue if it is Mac or Linux (since the command is run on the other end of `ssh`, it would be: Linux)

Like: the subtle difference between running the local client remotely, and running the remote client locally

When the VM is "hidden", then it can get very confusing. As we have seen from Docker Desktop and from WSL2.

----

Get's even worse with e.g. like "nerdctl.lima" that wraps "lima nerdctl" that wraps "limactl shell default nerdctl".

Makes even `podman` and `podman-remote` and `podman --remote` look straightforward, in comparison... :-)
I like the thinking, I've found the remote client/macOS bit to be confusing.  I just don't want us to go too strong and make people think we're not Windows too.
